data.title: The USDC's market capitalization is nearing an all-time high of $80 billion, which analysts say is related to capital outflows from the UAE.
data.description: USDC's circulating market capitalization has risen to approximately $79.2 billion, nearing its all-time high. The stablecoin's market capitalization has been steadily climbing since early February, when it was around $70 billion, and surpassed $75 billion earlier this month. Rami Al-Hashimi, who identifies himself as a Dubai analyst, links this surge in demand to capital outflows from the UAE. He states that current demand for USDC on Dubai's over-the-counter (OTC) platforms is struggling to meet demand, attributing this to the dramatic fluctuations in the local real estate market. According to him, Dubai property prices have fallen by approximately 27% this month. TradingView data shows that the DFM Real Estate Index, which tracks listed real estate and construction companies in Dubai, has fallen from a recent high of approximately 16,800 points to approximately 11,516 points, a drop of about 31%. Al-Hashimi also stated that some property sellers have begun accepting cryptocurrency payments and are offering discounts of 5% to 10% to buyers paying with BTC. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Iran Asserts Right to Target US Missile Sites in UAE
data.description: An Iranian military spokesperson declared on the 14th local time that Iran considers itself entitled to target US missile launch sites located in the United Arab Emirates. According to NS3.AI, this statement was made by a representative from the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters of the Iranian Armed Forces.

data.title: Weekend Show Streams Live on Bloomberg
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that their new weekend show is currently streaming live. The program, titled 'Bloomberg This Weekend,' is available for viewers to watch and listen to throughout the morning. The show aims to provide comprehensive coverage of the latest developments in business and finance, offering insights and analysis from industry experts. This initiative is part of Bloomberg's ongoing efforts to enhance its media offerings and engage with a broader audience.

data.title: Germany Urged to Speed Up Renewable Energy Adoption
data.description: Germany's environment minister has called for the country to hasten its transition to renewable energy sources to enhance energy security. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the minister's emphasis on the importance of renewable energy in reducing reliance on external energy supplies. The minister's appeal comes amid growing concerns over energy security and the need for sustainable energy solutions. The push for renewable energy is seen as a crucial step in ensuring a stable and secure energy future for Germany.

data.title: Boris Johnson Expresses Concerns Over Bitcoin's Structure
data.description: Former UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson recently shared his apprehensions about Bitcoin on social media, suggesting that his longstanding belief that Bitcoin resembles a Ponzi scheme might be accurate. According to NS3.AI, Johnson pointed to Bitcoin's decentralized nature, lack of intrinsic value, reliance on trust, and the high costs associated with its complex fee structures as reasons for his concerns.

data.title: USDC Market Cap Nears Record High Amid Middle East Demand Surge
data.description:
The market capitalization of the USDC stablecoin is nearing a record high of approximately $80 billion, driven by increasing demand in the Middle East. According to Cointelegraph, this surge is linked to capital flight from the United Arab Emirates, as investors seek alternatives to traditional markets. Data from CoinMarketCap indicates that USDC's circulating supply has reached around $79.2 billion, surpassing its previous peak of under $79 billion in December last year. The stablecoin's market cap has expanded significantly in recent weeks, rising from just over $70 billion in early February to $75 billion earlier this month.

Dubai-based analyst Rami Al-Hashimi attributes the surge in USDC demand to a downturn in the UAE's real estate market. He noted that property prices in Dubai have plummeted by approximately 27% this month, prompting investors to shift capital into digital assets. Al-Hashimi highlighted that over-the-counter (OTC) desks in Dubai are struggling to meet the growing demand for USDC. He also mentioned that some property sellers are now accepting cryptocurrency payments directly, offering discounts for buyers who pay with Bitcoin. This trend underscores the increasing appeal of digital assets during times of financial uncertainty.

In a related development, Japanese investment bank Mizuho reports that USDC has overtaken Tether's USDt in adjusted transaction volume for the first time since 2019. The bank's research indicates that USDC recorded about $2.2 trillion in adjusted transaction volume year-to-date, compared to $1.3 trillion for USDt, giving USDC approximately 64% of the combined transaction share. Despite this shift in transaction activity, USDt remains the largest stablecoin by market capitalization, standing at about $184 billion, significantly ahead of USDC's $79 billion.

data.title: China Criticizes Taiwan President's Democracy and Sovereignty Remarks
data.description: China's Taiwan Affairs Office has dismissed comments made by Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te, who associated democracy with sovereignty. Bloomberg posted on X, stating that the office described Lai's argument as misleading. The response highlights ongoing tensions between China and Taiwan over issues of governance and autonomy.

data.title: BTC has remained below $100,000 for 120 days.
data.description: Cointelegraph reported on its X platform that Bitcoin has been below $100,000 for over 120 days.

data.title: Iran Controls Kharg Island Situation After Attack
data.description: Iran has announced that the situation on Kharg Island is under control following an attack on its defense systems. According to Jin10, the deputy governor of Iran's Bushehr Province stated that the island was targeted by the United States and Israel on March 13, resulting in damage to some military facilities and Kharg Airport. Despite the attack, the island's oil facilities remain intact, and oil exports continue as usual. Businesses stationed on the island have not experienced any disruptions. The overall situation on Kharg Island remains calm, with daily life proceeding safely and orderly. The deputy governor confirmed that there were no casualties among military personnel, oil company employees, or island residents, and all departments are functioning normally.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum surpasses $2,167, the cumulative liquidation of short positions could reach $1.048 billion. Conversely, if Ethereum falls below $1,965, the liquidation of long positions could amount to $458 million.

data.title: India Expands LPG Supply to Industrial Sectors in Select States
data.description: India has initiated the supply of commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinders to industrial sectors in certain states, according to Jin10. This move is part of the government's efforts to bolster industrial growth and energy accessibility across the nation. The decision aims to enhance operational efficiency and support the energy needs of industries, contributing to economic development. The expanded LPG supply is expected to facilitate smoother industrial operations and potentially attract more investments into these regions. The government continues to focus on energy solutions that drive industrial progress and sustainability.

data.title: Gulf Nations' Investment Tied to Middle East Stability
data.description: Gulf nations have made significant investment pledges to the United States, with an implicit understanding to maintain stability in the Middle East, according to an economist. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic importance of these investments in fostering regional peace. The economist suggests that these financial commitments are part of a broader diplomatic effort to ensure that tensions in the Middle East do not escalate. This approach underscores the interconnected nature of economic and geopolitical interests between the Gulf countries and the U.S.

data.title: A federal court halted the Justice Department's subpoena investigation into the current Federal Reserve Chairman, significantly increasing the likelihood that Elizabeth Warren would support Warsh for the position.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that the probability of "Will Elizabeth Warren vote for Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair?" has risen from 1.9% to 28.0%. A recent ruling by a U.S. District Court has halted the Justice Department's subpoena investigation into current Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell. Previously, Warren, the top Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee, had clearly stated that the confirmation of a new Federal Reserve Chair should not proceed until the Trump administration completely halts the criminal investigations into Powell and Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook. Following the ruling, Warren called the investigation "political persecution" and, together with Republican Senator Thom Tillis, called on the Justice Department to completely abandon the investigation. With the investigation stalled, the preconditions it previously set are being met, significantly reducing the obstacles to Warsh's nomination. It is worth noting that Warren is known for her strong regulatory stance, while Warsh is more hawkish and favors deregulation; their policy positions are clearly opposed. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before they are priced in.

data.title: Iran to Increase Use of Upgraded Weapons, Defense Ministry Announces
data.description: Iran's Defense Ministry has announced plans to enhance the use of upgraded weapons, according to Iranian state media. The spokesperson for the ministry highlighted the strategic importance of this move, emphasizing its role in strengthening national defense capabilities. This development comes amid ongoing regional tensions and reflects Iran's commitment to advancing its military technology. The announcement did not specify which weapons would be upgraded or the timeline for these enhancements.

data.title: Binance Alpha 7th Katana (KAT) Prime Sale Pre-TGE user subscription limit is 3 BNB.
data.description: According to the official announcement, the maximum subscription per user for Binance Alpha's seventh Katana (KAT) Prime Sale Pre-TGE is 3 BNB. Users can claim a KAT Key (BSC) after the Pre-TGE phase ends, and KAT tokens will be airdropped to their Alpha account on the TGE day. Previously, it was announced that the subscription period for this event was from 20:00 to 22:00 on March 16th. Eligible users must participate using Alpha Points. Binance will list Katana (KAT) spot trading and add a seed tag. The listing time is 21:00 (UTC+8) on March 18th.

data.title: Iran Sets Conditions for Ending Conflict
data.description: Iran has outlined specific conditions under which it would consider ending its ongoing conflict. According to Jin10, Major General Mohsen Rezaei, a senior commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, stated that Iran would contemplate halting hostilities if two conditions are met: the recovery of all its losses and the withdrawal of the United States from the Persian Gulf. This announcement highlights the ongoing tensions in the region and the complex geopolitical dynamics at play.

data.title: Hedge Funds Increase Bullish Crude Oil Positions to Highest Level Since 2020
data.description: Hedge funds have significantly increased their bullish positions in crude oil, reaching the highest level since 2020. According to NS3.AI, ICE Futures Europe data for the week of March 10 indicates that the crude oil market is entering one of its most volatile periods on record. This surge in bullish positioning reflects heightened investor confidence in the potential for oil prices to rise amid ongoing market fluctuations.

data.title: Macron Urges Ceasefire Talks Between Israel and Lebanon
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced on Saturday via X platform that France is prepared to facilitate ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Lebanon in Paris. According to Jin10, Macron stated that Lebanon's leadership is open to direct talks with Israel. He urged Israel to seize this opportunity to initiate ceasefire discussions aimed at finding a lasting solution and enabling Lebanese authorities to fulfill their commitments to Lebanon's sovereignty. Macron also called on Israel to halt its offensive and urged Hezbollah to cease its actions. He emphasized the importance of preventing Lebanon from descending into chaos.

data.title: Iran's Oil Exports Continue Uninterrupted Despite Attack
data.description: Iran's oil exports from Kharg Island remain unaffected despite a recent attack, according to Jin10. An Iranian senior provincial official confirmed that operations are proceeding as usual, ensuring the continuity of oil shipments from the region. The official emphasized that the attack did not disrupt the flow of oil exports, maintaining stability in the country's oil trade.

data.title: UK Government Plans Targeted Support for Energy Bills
data.description: The UK government is set to introduce targeted assistance for low-income households struggling with rising energy costs, according to Chancellor Rachel Reeves. Bloomberg posted on X that this initiative aims to alleviate the financial burden on poorer British families as energy prices continue to climb. The government is focusing on providing relief to those most affected by the increasing costs, ensuring that support reaches the households in greatest need. This move comes amid growing concerns over the impact of energy expenses on the cost of living for vulnerable populations.
